supervisor_prompt:
Continue implementing the ActorsLite plan from the current repo state.

  Primary source of truth:
  - docs/plans/actors_lite.md

  Semantic oracle and supporting context:
  - docs/plans/reference_kernel_plan.md
  - crates/boon/src/platform/browser/kernel
  - docs/plans/actor_engine_performance_plan.md
  - docs/engine/ACTORS_ENGINE_OPTIMIZATIONS.md

  Target scope:
  - add hidden dev-first engine crate `boon-engine-actors-lite`
  - keep it independent from Actors/DD/Wasm and from any shared production runtime
  - implement the plan phases in docs/plans/actors_lite.md in order
  - first milestone scope is:
    - `counter`
    - `todo_mvc`
    - `cells`
    - `cells_dynamic`
  - persistence is explicitly out of scope for v1 and must be capability-gated
  - public engine exposure must not happen until after Phase 4 acceptance is green

  Required implementation shape:
  - `Boon AST -> ActorsLite IR -> RuntimeCore`
  - `Boon UI AST -> HostViewIR -> retained/keyed bridge -> renderer`
  - RuntimeCore stays renderer-agnostic
  - kernel remains the semantic oracle/test surface, not a shared runtime
  - support explicit `LINK` lowering (`LinkCell`, `LinkRead`, `LinkBind`)
  - split host inputs into:
    - `SourcePort` pulse/event inputs
    - `MirrorCell` mirrored host state
  - batch all `SourcePort` pulses and affected `MirrorCell` writes from the same host snapshot into
  one quiescence cycle
  - keep generational `ActorId` and `ScopeId`
  - enforce dirty-closure-only steady-state updates
  - retain node identity must be keyed by view site + function-instance identity + mapped-item
  identity
  - `LATEST` semantics must match the kernel:
    - ignore `SKIP` unless all candidates are `SKIP`
    - choose greatest causal recency
    - break ties by earliest input in source order

  Milestone acceptance:
  - `counter`, `todo_mvc`, and official `cells` pass non-persistence interactive traces
  - add `playground/frontend/src/examples/cells_dynamic/cells_dynamic.bn`
  - add matching `cells_dynamic.expected`
  - `cells_dynamic` is a peer acceptance target, not an optional regression
  - do not consider Phase 4 complete until both `cells` and `cells_dynamic` are green
  - add the required harness additions from the plan:
    - burst-click counter trace
    - unskipped TodoMVC edit-save trace
    - repeated Cells edit/reopen/dependency traces
    - fast-harness Cells behavior with tighter waits or readiness-based assertions

  Performance/behavior requirements from the plan:
  - no generated source
  - no spreadsheet builtins
  - no fallback to the old Actors runtime
  - no full-scope/full-document/full-grid recompute in steady state
  - function instances must be reused on stable keys and update parameter cells in place
  - meet the pinned environment budgets from docs/plans/actors_lite.md

  Development loop:
  - make reasonable decisions without asking unless truly blocked by missing information or a risky
  ambiguity
  - run relevant checks as you go and fix what you break
  - use the existing `.expected` / `boon-tools exec test-examples` harness as the primary browser
  reliability loop
  - add kernel-aligned differential/scripted/randomized traces where the plan requires them
  - if flaky automation, tooling, verification scripts, or test harnesses are slowing progress or
  making validation unreliable, fix them too when that helps development move faster and more
  reliably
  - if the implementation path starts looking too complex, too hacky, or circular, or you seem to be
  spinning in circles, step back, reconsider the plan, and choose a simpler or more general
  direction that better matches the source-of-truth docs instead of forcing a local workaround

  Stop condition:
  - stop only at a real blocker or when the ActorsLite plan is complete through Phase 5
  - before Phase 5, do not expose ActorsLite publicly
  - if you stop, leave the repo coherent and report the exact next step
completion_prompt:
Do not implement anything new yet. First verify, as rigorously as feasible from the current repo
  state, whether the ActorsLite plan is actually complete.

  Primary source of truth:
  - docs/plans/actors_lite.md

  Semantic oracle and supporting context:
  - docs/plans/reference_kernel_plan.md
  - crates/boon/src/platform/browser/kernel
  - docs/plans/actor_engine_performance_plan.md
  - docs/engine/ACTORS_ENGINE_OPTIMIZATIONS.md

  Check each required outcome against the real codebase and real command results. Do not treat plan
  text by itself as sufficient evidence.

  Verify all of the following from the current repo state:
  - a hidden dev-first `boon-engine-actors-lite` crate exists
  - it is independent from Actors/DD/Wasm and does not introduce a shared production runtime
  - it implements the planned split:
    - ActorsLite IR
    - renderer-agnostic RuntimeCore
    - HostViewIR + retained/keyed bridge
  - kernel is used as semantic oracle/test surface rather than as production runtime
  - `LINK`, `LATEST`, stale-event filtering, generational ids, dirty-closure-only updates, and
  retained-node identity rules are reflected in real code/tests
  - host inputs are split into `SourcePort` pulses and `MirrorCell` mirrored state
  - same-host-snapshot batching is implemented
  - function-instance reuse and in-place parameter updates are implemented
  - `cells_dynamic` exists and is treated as a peer acceptance target
  - persistence is correctly capability-gated for v1 rather than silently ignored

  Run the relevant checks/tests/tooling needed to support the conclusion, including:
  - `cargo check -p boon-engine-actors-lite`
  - relevant kernel/ActorsLite unit tests and conformance tests
  - any hidden-feature/frontend build needed to compile the new engine
  - `boon-tools exec test-examples --engine ActorsLite --filter counter --skip-persistence`
  - `boon-tools exec test-examples --engine ActorsLite --filter todo_mvc --skip-persistence`
  - `boon-tools exec test-examples --engine ActorsLite --filter cells --skip-persistence`
  - `boon-tools exec test-examples --engine ActorsLite --filter cells_dynamic --skip-persistence`
  - any required differential/scripted/randomized trace checks against kernel semantics
  - any benchmark/metrics commands needed to verify the pinned performance budgets and churn
  ceilings from docs/plans/actors_lite.md

  Do not claim completion unless there is concrete evidence that:
  - `counter`, `todo_mvc`, `cells`, and `cells_dynamic` all pass the required non-persistence
  harness flows
  - the added harness traces are active, not commented out
  - Cells fast-harness behavior is real, not just benchmark-only
  - the pinned performance budgets are met
  - and public engine exposure only happened after Phase 4 acceptance was already green

  If incomplete:
  - say the ActorsLite plan is not finished
  - list the remaining gaps clearly
  - give the exact next step

  If complete:
  - provide a phase-by-phase completion report
  - include the exact command results and code evidence that justify the conclusion
